chunks.text: CS Major Lower Division Degree Requirements - EECS at Berkeley CS Major Lower Division Degree Requirements Lower Division Requirements All courses taken for the major must be taken for a letter grade. Students must complete all the lower-division cou

Students must complete all the lower-division course requirements with a passing letter grade to graduate. Math 51 (Calculus I) Math 52 (Calculus II) Math 54 or Math 56 or EECS 16A CS 61A CS 61B/BL CS 61C CS 70 Math 51 can be satisfied with at least

S 61C CS 70 Math 51 can be satisfied with at least a “3” on the AP AB or BC Calculus exam, at least a “5” on the IB Math Higher Level, or at least a “B” on A-Level Math IB Math, Analysis and Approaches (Higher Level): A score of 5, 6, or 7 can be use

s (Higher Level): A score of 5, 6, or 7 can be used to satisfy Math 51 or 16A. A score of 7 can be used to satisfy Math 51 and Math 52. Math 52 can be satisfied with a “5” on the AP BC Calculus exam or at least a “B” on A-Level Further Math. IB Math

or at least a “B” on A-Level Further Math. IB Math, Analysis and Approaches (Higher Level): A score of 5, 6, or 7 can be used to satisfy Math 51 or 16A. A score of 7 can be used to satisfy Math 51 and Math 52. Math 54 (Linear Algebra and Differentia

d Math 52. Math 54 (Linear Algebra and Differential Equations) or Math 56 (Linear Algebra) or EECS 16A (Foundations of Signals, Dynamical Systems, and Information Processing); Math 54 or equivalent is a prerequisite for taking EECS 16A. Beginning Fal

a prerequisite for taking EECS 16A. Beginning Fall 2026, EECS 16A may no longer fulfill linear algebra for CS. If taken at a CA community college, Computer Science does not require full equivalence to Math 54 and will accept just the Linear Algebra

o Math 54 and will accept just the Linear Algebra course of an articulated Math 54-equivalent series. CS 61A (Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs) CS 61B/BL (Data Structures) CS 61C (Machine Structures) CS 70 (Discrete Mathematics and P

hine Structures) CS 70 (Discrete Mathematics and Probability Theory) Math 55 is accepted for math/cs double majors, but CS 70 is recommended. The math grade will be used in the GPA calculation. Students need to be declared in math first. Resources In
